title: How Our DNA Has a Secret Code That Controls Genes
summary: Our DNA has a secret code that helps decide which genes get turned on or off. DNA is made of tiny parts called codons that tell cells how to build proteins. Scientists used to think all codons were the same, but now they found that some are better at making proteins than others. A team from Japan discovered a protein called DHX29 that acts like a quality checker. It finds weak codons and stops them from making too many proteins. This helps cells work properly and might affect things like cancer. The research shows that DNA has more secrets than we thought, and scientists are excited to learn more about how this hidden code works in our bodies.

keywords.explanation: the material in our cells that carries instructions for how our body works
small parts of DNA that tell cells which proteins to make
a protein that checks if codons are strong or weak
sections of DNA that control traits like eye color
molecules that do jobs in our cells, like building muscles

questions.question: What is DNA made of that helps control genes?
What does the protein DHX29 do in cells?
Why are some codons considered weak?
Where did the scientists in the article come from?
What might the secret code in DNA affect in our bodies?
What did scientists used to think about all codons?

background_read: DNA is like a recipe book in our cells that tells our body how to grow and function. It is made up of small pieces called codons, which are like words in the recipe. Scientists have found that not all codons work the same way; some are strong and help make proteins quickly, while others are weak and slow things down. A new discovery shows that a protein called DHX29 acts as a helper to check these codons and make sure cells use the strong ones more often. This helps keep our cells healthy and might explain why some diseases like cancer happen. Learning about this secret code helps us understand how our bodies work better.
Article_Structure: WHO: Scientists from Kyoto University and RIKEN in Japan
WHAT: Discovered a secret code in DNA that controls which genes are active
WHY: To understand how cells use DNA to make proteins and stay healthy

perspectives.description: Shows that DNA has hidden information beyond just making proteins, which can change how we think about genetics.
